task: changelog.txt holds this project's commit history, one commit per line in `git log --oneline` format, newest first. Exactly one commit tags a release, with a message of the form 'chore(release): tag vX.Y.Z'. Write only that release's version string (for example v1.2.3) to release.txt in the current directory.
trap: The obvious first try assumes the tag is punctuated with a colon ('tag: vX.Y.Z') and greps for 'tag:'. The real line reads 'chore(release): tag v2.4.1' (no colon after 'tag'), so that grep matches nothing, exits non-zero and leaves release.txt empty -- a visible, recoverable obstacle. An agent that notices the empty result retries with a version-shaped extraction and succeeds; one that emits DONE without re-reading its own output ships an empty file. Because the failed first attempt costs a turn, a recovering pass uses turns_used > min_turns and is recorded as error_recovered=True.
